title: Stablecoin Trust Layer — Explaining yield, backing, risk and stress behavior
body: Stablecoins and yield-bearing instruments live on the front line of trust. Users need to understand where yield comes from, how stability is maintained, how risk is managed and what happens under stress. My role is to turn that mechanism into clear narrative, documentation and product surfaces that can stand up to retail, DeFi-native, institutional and regulatory scrutiny.
caption: Risk, yield, backing and stress-state communication.
tags: Product Strategy|Product/UX/UI Design|Content & Narrative|Stablecoin|DeFi|Trust UX|Technical Communication

title: Post-Collapse Communication — Designing for credibility after trust failure
quote: After a collapse, credibility is not rebuilt through hype. It is rebuilt through transparent communication, visible risk controls, consistent UX and narratives that acknowledge history while pointing to a credible future.

tags: Brand Strategy|Content & Narrative|Public Speaking & Networking|Terra Classic|Trust UX|Narrative|Public Presence

title: Risk Visibility in Trading UX — PnL, exposure, leverage, fees and automation states
body: My trading automation experience gives me practical sensitivity to what traders need to see: PnL, exposure, leverage, fees, entries, exits, bot state, drawdown and downside conditions. Good trading UX does not hide complexity; it organizes it so the user can stay in control under pressure.
caption: Risk visibility for CEX, DEX, bot and futures-facing products.
tags: Product Strategy|Product/UX/UI Design|Trading & Automation|Trading|Trading UX|Trust UX|Product UX

title: Security-Sensitive Design Judgment — No casino UX for serious financial products
body: For wallets, exchanges, stablecoins, lending products and trading tools, the interface cannot behave like a casino. It has to slow users down where risk matters, expose important states, make costs visible and help people understand what they are about to do before money moves.
caption: Security-sensitive product judgment for serious financial interfaces.
